content.problemStatement:
Chef is an advocate for Go Green Initiative. Today he had **n** trees planted in a row outside his his restaurant. Today, the height of **i**-th tree is **hi** feet. The trees grow at a rate of **mi** feet per day.

Chef knows that trees will look beautiful if they form a zig-zag sequence. The trees will be said to be in Zig-zag sequence if the heights of tree first increases or decreases, then alternates between decreasing/increasing respectively. Formally, the trees will be said to in Zig-zag sequence if one of the following two conditions holds.



**h1 < h2 > h3 < h4** and so on..
**h1 >  h2 < h3 > h4** and so on..


Chef wants to know intervals of time when the heights of the trees will form a zig-zag sequence.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N, the number of trees, followed by N lines, each containing two space-separated integers hi and mi, the initial height and the growth speed for the ith tree.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of intervals of consecutive moments of time when the trees form a zig-zag sequence, followed by the intervals of time when the trees' heights form a zig-zag sequence. For each interval, print its smallest and the largest instants of time. If the range is infinite, output Inf as the right bound.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has a zig-zag sequence from time 0 to 1. The second test case has a zig-zag sequence from time 0 to infinity. The third test case does not have any zig-zag sequences.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the intervals of time when the trees form a zig-zag sequence. We can do this by checking all possible intervals of time and verifying if the trees form a zig-zag sequence during that interval. We can use the fact that the height of each tree at time t is given by hi + mi * t, where hi is the initial height and mi is the growth speed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n^2) per test case, O(T * n^2) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — the dominant structure is the array of tree heights and growth speeds.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n = int(input())
    h = [0] * n
    m = [0] * n
    for i in range(n):
        h[i], m[i] = map(int, input().split())
    Q = 0
    print(Q)
    for i in range(n - 1):
        if (h[i] < h[i + 1] and m[i] > m[i + 1]) or (h[i] > h[i + 1] and m[i] < m[i + 1]):
            Q += 1
            print(0, 0)
